skill.name: debug-connection
skill.description: Guides troubleshooting for WebSocket sync issues between a Figram CLI server and a FigJam plugin.
skill.summary: Debug FigJam WebSocket connection, sync, import, and patch delivery issues.
skill.author: 7nohe

security_audit.summary: The static external-command detections are mostly true as documentation examples, not executable skill code. The weak cryptography detections at SKILL.md lines 3, 75, 76, and 100 are false positives with no cryptographic implementation found. Publishing is acceptable with a warning because the skill instructs users to run local diagnostic commands that may expose process, port, and verbose log information.

medium_findings.title: Local Diagnostic Commands May Reveal Environment Details
medium_findings.description: The skill asks users to run process, port, Bun CLI, DEBUG, and WebSocket test commands. These are legitimate troubleshooting steps, but output may reveal process names, local ports, file paths, document identifiers, or verbose application logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in Markdown examples and include process, port, DEBUG, and WebSocket diagnostics. They are not executed by the skill itself, so the concern is operational exposure rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography at lines 3, 75, 76, and 100, but those lines contain descriptive text, JSON field requirements, or console instructions. No hashing, encryption, or weak cryptographic algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms the flagged locations do not contain cryptographic code or algorithm names. The alerts appear caused by substring matches in documentation text.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: The skill file does not contain text that claims system authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, or requests changes to security analysis. No prompt injection attempt was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full SKILL.md content was reviewed and consists of troubleshooting documentation. No suspicious override, authority, or analysis-skipping language was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples for process inspection, port inspection, CLI serving, verbose logging, and WebSocket testing. Users should review commands before running them and avoid sharing sensitive command output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and semantically tied to local diagnostics. The risk is bounded because the file is documentation and does not include automation that runs them.

content.value_statement: FigJam plugin sync failures can be hard to isolate across CLI, UI, and plugin code. This skill gives a structured diagnostic path for WebSocket connection and patch issues.
actual_capabilities: Explains the CLI, plugin UI, and plugin main message flow.
Lists checks for connection refused and dropped WebSocket sessions.
Guides troubleshooting for patch delivery and canvas update failures.
Documents YAML and JSON validation symptoms for import failures.
Provides local command examples for ports, CLI serve, and WebSocket tests.
Defines the expected hello, full sync, patch, and error message shapes.
limitations: It does not run diagnostics or inspect a repository by itself.
It assumes the Figram CLI and FigJam plugin architecture described in the file.
It does not replace secure handling of secrets or sensitive debug logs.
It does not verify production network or browser policy issues.

use_cases.title: Resolve Plugin Connection Failures
use_cases.description: Diagnose why a FigJam plugin stays in a connecting state or cannot reach the local CLI server.

use_cases.title: Investigate Sync Drops
use_cases.description: Trace WebSocket disconnects, YAML parse failures, and missing updates across the CLI and plugin UI.

use_cases.title: Validate Message Flow
use_cases.description: Compare expected hello, full sync, patch, and error messages against observed WebSocket behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Check Connection Refused
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the debug-connection skill to help me diagnose a FigJam plugin that stays on Connecting. Ask for only the command output needed.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Dropped Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the debug-connection skill to trace a WebSocket session that connects at first, then stops syncing diagram updates.

prompt_templates.title: Review Patch Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the debug-connection skill to compare my observed WebSocket messages with the expected full sync and patch message flow.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Import Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the debug-connection skill to diagnose a JSON import error and identify which DSL or IR document requirement is missing.

output_examples.input: The plugin says Connecting and never updates.
output_examples.output: The assistant narrows the issue to server availability, confirms the expected port, checks whether the CLI serve process is running, and suggests restarting the CLI.

output_examples.input: The canvas does not update after a YAML change.
output_examples.output: The assistant separates CLI patch generation, plugin UI receipt, and plugin main rendering so the failing stage can be identified.

output_examples.input: My import dialog reports a validation error.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks whether the imported document is DSL or IR format and points to the missing document fields.

best_practices: Run diagnostic commands locally and review output before sharing it.
Check CLI logs, plugin UI console, and plugin main console separately.
Use a temporary secret for local testing and avoid pasting real secrets into chat.
anti_patterns: Do not expose full DEBUG logs if they include tokens, file paths, or private document data.
Do not assume the WebSocket is healthy only because the plugin opened.
Do not skip YAML or JSON validation when sync messages stop arriving.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands that a user may choose to run during local troubleshooting.

faq.question: What problem does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on WebSocket connection and sync issues between the Figram CLI and a FigJam plugin.

faq.question: Can it debug YAML parse errors?
faq.answer: It can guide checks for YAML validation problems that block update generation.

faq.question: Does it cover authentication problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a secret mismatch symptom and tells users to compare CLI and plugin secret values.

faq.question: Is it safe to share all command output?
faq.answer: No. Review process lists, verbose logs, and WebSocket messages for secrets or private data first.

faq.question: Can it inspect Figma plugin internals directly?
faq.answer: No. It tells users where to inspect plugin UI and plugin main console output.
